headline_draft: Perspective AI: running 100+ customer interviews per quarter without a dedicated researcher is now feasible
summary: Perspective AI released a ranking of AI research-stack tools for PMs operating without dedicated researchers. The core insight: 70% of PMs now run discovery solo, and Perspective AI is the only tool in the research stack that conducts customer conversations autonomously (not just analyzing data collected elsewhere). The tool runs hundreds of interviews in parallel and ships a ranked synthesis the same day. This compression changes the economics of customer discovery: a PM can now cost-effectively run quarterly primary research at scale (100+ interviews) that previously required hiring a contractor or researcher. The research stack splits into four lanes: conversational discovery (Perspective AI), feedback aggregation (Enterpret, Anecdote), session replay (FullStory, LogRocket), and roadmap validation (Sprig, Maze).
why_pm: Discovery velocity just became a competitive lever you control. If customer conversations are no longer a bottleneck, you can validate product ideas weekly instead of quarterly, run competitive research on demand, and reduce reliance on external research partners. The task shifts from 'finding time to talk to customers' to 'interpreting what the data tells you'—which is exactly where PM judgment should live.
key_facts: 70% of PMs in 2026 run discovery without dedicated researchers
Perspective AI autonomously conducts customer interviews and ships synthesis same day
Can run 100+ interviews per quarter within typical PM workload
Primary research no longer requires hiring contractors or researchers
Research stack splits into: conversational discovery, feedback aggregation, session replay, roadmap validation
quotes: Choose Perspective AI as your primary AI research tool if you are a product manager running discovery, validation, or PMF research without a dedicated researcher—which is the default situation for over 70% of PMs in 2026.

sources.label: Perspective AI: Best AI Tools by Workflow Stage

headline_draft: GPT-5.6 rumored for late June release: Prediction markets price 80-89% odds; DevEx implications for PM roadmapping
summary: As of June 14, 2026, GPT-5.6 remains unannounced by OpenAI but has become the subject of intense speculation. Polymarket traders are pricing 80-89% odds of a release by June 30, 2026. Evidence includes Codex backend log leaks referencing 'gpt-5.6' (with internal codenames 'ember-alpha' and 'beacon-alpha'), reports of ChatGPT Pro users testing versions with up to 1.5M token context (43% increase over GPT-5.5), and historical release cadence patterns suggesting a ~60-day iteration cycle. If confirmed, expected improvements include deeper long-context reasoning, better error recovery in agentic workflows, and improved performance on benchmarks like Terminal-Bench and GPQA. This creates a window of uncertainty for PMs building on GPT-5.5 or planning feature rollouts.
why_pm: If GPT-5.6 launches by June 30 as predicted, it could shift capability baselines mid-quarter and force feature decisions. PMs hardcoding GPT-5.5 into their products face technical debt if GPT-5.6 becomes the standard within weeks. The broader lesson: using model abstraction layers and version-agnostic routing is now a PM risk-management practice, not just an engineering elegance. Additionally, this release velocity (6 weeks between GPT-5.5 and 5.6) suggests frontier labs are in a race mode that will compress feature windows. Your roadmap needs to account for rapid model shifts.
key_facts: GPT-5.6 not officially announced as of June 14, 2026
Polymarket odds: 80-89% for release by June 30, 2026
Evidence: Codex log leaks, ChatGPT Pro 1.5M token context probes, 60-day cadence pattern
Expected: ~43% larger context window, improved agentic reasoning, better error recovery
Release window: mid-to-late June 2026 (if on cadence)
quotes: GPT-5.6 is real-but-not-yet-announced. The Codex log leak proves internal existence. Polymarket consensus pegs late June 2026 as the most likely outcome.
If GPT-5.6 ships with the expected ~6-week cadence, expect API access on launch day for tier-3+ developer accounts and gated rollout for the Pro variant—same pattern as GPT-5.5.

This is one of the most compressed release windows in frontier-model history. For builders, the practical consequence is that whatever you pick today may not be the right choice in 30 days—model routing and abstraction layers matter more in June 2026 than at any prior point this year.
